title: Sprint timing built by an Olympian
intro: TrackSpeed was born on the track. Sondre Guttormsen, a two-time Olympian and software developer, got tired of inaccurate timing tools, so he built his own.
story1: As a track and field athlete training at the highest level, Sondre needed accurate timing for sprints and approach runs, but the options were either expensive laser gate systems with inconsistent trigger points or a coach with a stopwatch and human reaction time error. Neither was good enough.
story2: He realized that the phone in everyone's pocket already had the hardware to solve this: a high frame rate camera, precise gyroscope, and enough processing power for real-time computer vision. So he built TrackSpeed: a timing system that uses frame-by-frame motion detection and sub-frame interpolation to deliver millisecond-accurate results, no extra equipment needed.

sondre.name: Sondre Guttormsen
sondre.bio1: Professional pole vaulter and two-time Olympian (Tokyo 2020, Paris 2024) representing Norway. NCAA champion at Princeton University, where he tied the all-time collegiate indoor record at 6.00m. Holds Ivy League records in both indoor and outdoor pole vault. Gold medalist at the European Athletics Indoor Championships.
sondre.bio2: Founder & Lead Developer of TrackSpeed. Princeton B.A., UT Austin M.Ed.

cta.title: Ready to time your training?
cta.subtitle: Download TrackSpeed and experience millisecond precision with just your phone.
cta.learnMore: Learn how our timing technology works

metadata.title: About TrackSpeed — Sprint Timing Built by an Olympian
metadata.description: Meet Sondre Guttormsen, founder and lead developer of TrackSpeed, building precision sprint timing for every athlete.
